title: geranium vagans subsp. whytei
intro: Geranium vagans subsp. whytei, also known as the Whyte's geranium, is a subspecies of flowering plant native to the island of Bermuda. It is a succulent plant with medicinal properties, primarily used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent qualities. The Whyte's geranium is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor cuts.
